instructions.instruction: Select the criteria format based on the user's request — use Given/When/Then (Gherkin) when clarity of sequence matters, or rule-oriented format when the user explicitly requests it.
All acceptance criteria must express measurable, observable outcomes — never vague language such as 'should be fast', 'user-friendly', or 'works correctly'.
original_snippets: NEVER use vague, non-measurable criteria. WHY: vague language cannot be tested objectively.
BAD: 'The system should be fast.' GOOD: 'Search returns in <= 2s for 95% of requests.'

instructions.instruction: Always include negative and boundary scenarios alongside the happy path — covering invalid inputs, error states, timeouts, and edge conditions.
original_snippets: NEVER skip negative and boundary scenarios. WHY: real users hit error and edge conditions.
BAD: only valid-login scenario listed. GOOD: include invalid credentials, timeout, and expired session behavior.

instructions.instruction: Include an explicit Out of Scope section to define scope boundaries and prevent scope creep.
original_snippets: Scope clear: password reset flow only, not account recovery via SMS.

instructions.instruction: Never write implementation instructions as acceptance criteria — criteria must describe outcomes, not technology or code choices.
original_snippets: NEVER write implementation instructions as acceptance criteria. WHY: criteria define outcomes, not implementation details.
BAD: 'Use JavaScript validation on submit button click.' GOOD: 'Invalid form input shows error message within 1 second.'

instructions.instruction: Apply INVEST checks (Independent, Negotiable, Valuable, Estimable, Small, Testable) when asked to review or rewrite a user story.
original_snippets: Apply INVEST quality checks to this story.

instructions.instruction: Format acceptance criteria as checkbox items (- [ ] ...) under named sections such as 'Acceptance Criteria (Must Have)' and 'Negative/Edge Scenarios'.
Never couple multiple distinct behaviours into a single criterion — each criterion must address exactly one observable outcome.
original_snippets: NEVER couple multiple behaviors into one ambiguous criterion. WHY: broad criteria hide failure source and reduce test clarity.
BAD: 'Checkout and email and invoice should work.' GOOD: split into separate measurable criteria.

instructions.instruction: Every criterion must be independently pass/fail testable by product, engineering, and QA without further clarification.
original_snippets: Criteria are understandable by product + QA.

instructions.instruction: Capture user outcome and business intent before drafting criteria — ensure criteria trace back to a stated user benefit.
original_snippets: Capture user outcome and business intent.
User outcome: User regains account access after forgetting password. Business intent: Reduce support tickets; maintain security.
